title: Silky Sweet Venezuelan Fettuccine Pasta
description: A velvety, sweet pasta that blends the heartwarming comfort of Venezuelan flavors with a protein-packed twist, perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Rinse fettuccine and bring a large pot of salted water to a boil.
While the water heats, slice the plantain into 1/4‑inch rounds and toss with a pinch of salt.
Preheat the air fryer to 200 °C (400 °F). Place plantain slices in the basket in a single layer and air‑fry for 10 minutes, turning halfway, until golden and slightly crisp. Remove and set aside.

In a small saucepan, combine coconut milk, minced garlic, honey, coconut sugar, lime juice, salt, and pepper. Warm over medium heat until the sugar dissolves and the mixture thickens slightly, about 3 minutes. Set aside.
In the same pot of boiling pasta water, cook the fettuccine according to package instructions until al dente, about 8‑10 minutes; reserve 1/4 cup of pasta water and drain the pasta.
While the pasta cooks, heat a non‑stick skillet over medium heat and sauté the diced red bell pepper until just softened, about 2 minutes.
Add the cooked black beans, sliced chicken, and sautéed bell pepper to the skillet; stir to warm through.

Toss the drained fettuccine into the skillet with the bean‑chicken mixture, then pour the coconut‑milk sauce over the pasta. Add a splash of reserved pasta water if the sauce needs loosening, and stir until everything is evenly coated and silky.
Fold in the air‑fried plantain slices gently, reserving a few for garnish.
Plate the pasta, sprinkle with chopped cilantro, and serve immediately for a sweet, silky Venezuelan dinner.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
